channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake — 53 replies — 105.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-03-21 05:24 UTC

OP

I’ve been telling folks here not to sell BTC and now they’re buying at the top.

>When are you gonna learn your lesson, folks? HODL

Early replies (first 3)

61996853
>>61996755
rich kid but black

61996883
>>61996853
What's the problem?

61998635
>>61996755
Sell and wait for 10k per btc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003117
BTC might retrace a bit, but don’t panic sell. Plan your moves, don’t let fear drive trades.

62003136
>>62003088
It's 0.07% APR. And you will be sending your BTC to an anonymous system whose owner pinky promises to send you your money back if you ask nicely.

62003422
>>62003088
It’s possible, just look into it yourself. you’ll get it. Self custody is the advantage.

62003430
>>61996755
Let the goyim deal in the crypto world.

62003444
>>62003136
“Anonymous system” lol. It’s literally built around cryptographic guarantees, not trust. APR is low, sure, but better than nothing. This isn’t sending BTC to some random yield farm.

62003448
>>61998652
He's a great kiddo

62003660
>>62003430
Bitcoin keeps improving. Lightning, Taproot, and other upgrades make BTC faster, cheaper, and more usable, confidence grows, price follows.

62003668
Market sentiment drives crypto hard. A little media buzz, some whales buying, and suddenly BTC is pumping. FOMO is real.

62003679
>>62003444
The only defi right now is ethereum

62003695
>>62003117
Whats your price target?

62003910
>>62003679
“Only Ethereum” is outdated. Solana and Sui already have real DeFi, and BTC is just getting started. Most of it is still sleeping, not because it can’t be used.

62003914
>>62003111
Smart guy for buying the dip

62004044
>>61996755
I am blaming Trump for cryoto dumps

62004059
your president is greedy ass

62004175
>>61996755
>Buy XRP and LINK instead

62004400
>>61996755
I need you guys to pump my vaporware up over $96,000 so that I can make my exit. you guys know how this works, so don't play dumb. your exit will come at some point in the future, trust the plan.

62005462
>>62003660
I guess you know better than I do.

62006331
>>62003668
You mean manipulation?

62006772
>>62004400
buy memecoins and enjoy

62007988
>>62004044
You mean the ongoing war conflict? I didn't sign up for that

62008412
>>62003910
How are we making money there?

62008424
>>61996755
BUY ICP

62008434
>>62008412
it's risky. Don't ever try

62010261
>>62008412
You can stake your BTC there natively. If you want more, better wait for their upcoming Trustless Bitcoin Vaults, they’ll open use cases like lending and more.
>>62008434
Risky how? You even know what you’re talking about?

62010279
>>62006772
Which memecoins? Lol

62010288
>>62004400
Then trust your plan ser

62010298
>>62008424
This coin is not really giving me profits

62010313
I've been DCAing since we dumped

62010369
That's right saar do not redeem the BTC 100k again very soon

62012799
>>62010313
That's nice ser

item.title: USA is removing sanctions on Iranian oil — 36 replies — 73.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
USA is removing sanctions on Iranian oil

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-21 05:24 UTC

OP

This is going to crash the oil market!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62005086
>>62005061
based bussyent

62005090
>>62005061
stupid, they should be forced to sell it to us for $$30 a barrel.

62005101
The message is that they fucked it up so hard they're begging the enemy they want to destroy to sell them OIL lmfao. Sure, faggot, what about a tariff for Amerimutts plus you pay in yuan, sodomite.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62005213
>>62005061
>dow is -1,02 % within 6 months timeframe
>no cuts
>oil is 100+
>no investments from the gulf
>the pool's closed
kwab

62005234
Oh my god they’re removing sanctions on 4% of the global oil production… we are saved!!!

Seriously what the fuck does this matter? Does he think the Iranians are going to sell us oil so we can bomb them with it?

62005243
>>62005234
>anyone in that retarded administration
>think

62005246
>treasury secretary implementing a policy with a 2 WEEK time preference

its so over

62005453
>>62005234
He and Israel know what they are doing. Keep destabilizing middle east, which will lead to refugees migrating to europe, which will in turn destabilize europe. At the same time, keep Russia as aggressive as possible. They don’t ever want to see late 90s-2008 style european living quality supremacy again.

62006698
>>62005061
Someone explain the reasoning. If China doesn't get those 140 million barrels, wouldn't they just buy from some other source, increasing demand and price for that source? How would that bring down the prices? It's not like these barrels were completely out of the market.

62006729
>>62006698
Yes. Over time, it's all fungible.

The most likely reason sanctions are being removed on Iranian, Russian and Belarusian commodities is because some countries with small stockpiles are now so desperate to buy fuels and fertilizers from the nearest ship that they are willing to ignore US sanctions, and so the US suspends sanctions to avoid the embarrassment of being unable to enforce sanctions

62006750
>>62005061
Lmao what a fucking joke. Desperation.

62008663
>>62005061
Is this a humiliation ritual? If they were going to embarrass themselves like this they should never have started the war to begin with. What a fuckup.

62008682
>>62005234
Its not like Iran couldnt make use of the payments.

62008783
>>62006729
Sounds accurate

62008815
>>62008663
>Is this a humiliation ritual? If they were going to embarrass themselves like this
this is the trump presidency in a nutshell.

62008854
>>62008682
The idea behind avoiding destruction of their oil is to leave them something to surrender to. If that was gone they would truly be on deaths ground with nothing left to lose. By leaving them something to lose, you increase leverage for a negotiated settlement. Without the oil they have 0 reason to negotiate, with there may be a situation where they say fuck this let's focus on the oil gains

62009078
>>62006698
>>62006729
>>62008854
3 out of 20
15% non indian posts
Grim

62012144
>>62005453
American geopolitics is mostly two thing.
1/ Destroy europa, and prevent the Berlin-Moscow axis. The more europa sink, the more america take over it's markets.
2/ Maintain the petrodollar, and crush anyone who dare sell it's oil not in dollars (Saddam Hussein, Gaddafhi, Maduro (end 2025), Iran...).

62012155
Bro, let us buy your oil so that we can bomb you, bro. We're totally winning, I'm tired of winning, bro, why are you bombing our bases, bro. Please, stop, we're winning, bro.

62012261
>>62005453
>>62012144
Believe it or not...no one really cares about Europe. Europe is a bunch of tourist destinations that play no significant role in the global economy.

What is the biggest European economy? Germany? And they've been nose diving without imports from other nations like Russia and without as big a Chinese market.

62012273
>>62012261
you would have no industry if it wasn't for all the highly technical products you buy from germany, france and netherlands

62012285
>>62005246
We already have venezuela's oil under American custodianship that is also severely hindering the world's trade as well. This is just a 2 week break to let the dust settle before the Russian and Chinese frog is boiled in even hotter water. If Iranian regime change is effectively successful, the United States literally controls the majority of trade. This also has massive implications on the reunification of Taiwan because if the Changs can't fuel their war machine, theyre SOL.

ALTHOUGH what im saying is purely speculative, but if Iran is beaten down enough and the IRGC is out of power, the Ukraine war is also over, due to attrition.

If Russia and China can't get illegal Iranian or Venezuelan oil. It's game over.

62012292
>>62012285
>We already have venezuela's oil under American custodianship that is also severely hindering the world's trade as well
ahahahahahaha. yeah, venezuela will magically fix their non-working oil machinery, re-import their lost expertise, and the shitty venezuelan oil will somehow be refined and distributed in 2 more weeks.

62012296
>>62012292
Thats also a valid assertion given that theyre ran by communists monkeys.

62012444
>>62005061
Trump is the greatest strategist of all time
and by strategist I mean the highest ranked dementia patient of all time

62012471
>>62005061
they're getting desperate

62012484
>>62005061
Wasn't China buying their oil anyway, so this would just mean China could just flip the Iran oil for a profit, what does this do

62012488
>>62012471
>A is the X; B is the Y
Slop detected + opinion discarded

62012490
>>62012471
>gold price will go up because mentally ill, everything-controlling lolbertarian techbros and shit
imagine believing this bullshit lmao

62012498
>>62005206
>what happens
Nukes if they keep going I imagine.

62012504
This reeks of sheer desperation.

62012787
>>62012471
This is some grade-A quality schizo theory and I love it.

62012801
>>62005061
this is meaningless rhetoric and I'm tired of this blatantly bullshit market manipulation every fucking week
the oil was only sanctioned to NATOcucks and Iran got their money in Chinese bank accounts which orange niggers couldn't touch even if he rimmed Netanyahu on an emergency broadcast
no NATOcuck country is going to touch Iranian oil and it will still flow to China so what he's saying is essentially "uhh don't worry guys we won't blow their oil up we know how fucked up things are" which is like NO FUCKING SHIT
I'm so fucking tired of this retarded nigger administration

item.title: Dollar = Doge — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Dollar = Doge

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-21 05:42 UTC

OP

I know, I know, Doge is a memcoin, but at this point so is the dollar. It's just a meme that's been around longer and has a stronger reputation. Well Dogecoin is steadily gaining a similar status whether you see it or not. If you could imagine it, why wouldn't Doge become the new Dollar. I mean it already shares the first two letters as the "Do"llar. It's got a strong community backing. The Goverment brought in a legitmate DOGE department. Elon Musk has already used it as a sort of gift shop trial run for Tesla. He even recently dropped another meme AI video of him as the Dogefather. Doge-1 space mission might be launching this year. DOGE was one of the 16 cryptos that made it to the list for Clarity or potential Clarity, as commodity. X payments going live in April, with rumors saying 4/20 Dogecoin implementation or maybe news update on its integration.

I'm sure there is even more things I'm missing, but bottomline, who truly knows the future of Crypto and which ones will take over. Dogecoin seems like it may be ramping up for something much bigger than others have precieved.

Early replies (first 3)

62009117
>>62009107
Doge will be the #2 coin soon. Doge is the new ETH killer.

62009134
>>62009107
Its unironically better money than USD
That's not a high bar, fiat is just such shit
What happened to all the crypto payment processor protocols (some advertising doge) that were supposed to be rolling out last year?
Its like the whole bullrun narrative (I.e. actually doing shit with the technologies we have available) hit the pause button because of political bullshit

62009147
>>62009117
That's a huge stretch, but it's in the number 9 slot on coinmarketcap and X payment integration could 100% skyrocket it. I hope so.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62009167
>>62009134
Fiat is shit, but was good when backed by gold. I agree it would be nice to see society using technology to its full potential, but I still think technology for the masses is a little too much to handle, especially Boomers, and in general money related technology needs lot of R&D testing to prove out the bugs.

62009223
>>62009147
It would only have to be $1.50~ to overtake ETH. And once you realize that Doge could literally triple in price 0.30 in a day or two from those big red short candles, 1.50 becomes really possible.

62009247
>>62009223
WoW, suppose you're right. I didn't think it was that close in comparison to ETH. This makes my thread post even more relevant to the potential of Dogecoin. Thanks for your response. This just re-enforces Dogecoin more.

62009312
>>62009107
DOGE is the silver to BTC gold.

62009330
>>62009312
I'm down with that! (^o^)

62009592
>>62009247
I believe in doge
Not because its DA BESTEST but because its ascent would be such a slap in the face to srsbzns projects that all got coopted and nerfed into the ground (like btc to an extent); mass adoption would be the funniest outcome, and therefore fitting for fake and gay clownworld

62010053
>>62009592
It really would be hilarious to see Doge or even Shib come out on top and be known as the common new Dollar and then all the people who missed the boat on it kicking themselves for passing up something so stupid. With how the world is and how silly it's become, Dogecoin as a US currency doesn't seem to far fetched.

62011089
>>62009117
CHECKED

62012826
>>62009107
They say that every doge has his day.

Holy fuck stockfags are fucking retarded.

62011671
Unironically it's gonna go back to 6000 at least before we bottom out. Luckily unlike in 2022 I have cash flow (thanks for fucking me, Biden) and a lot outside the market. Gonna just holla holla stack dolla until EOY when I should have 150k to throw at it. If you look at TQQQ in the 2022 lows to the highs, that's an easy 8x in 2-3 years. Then I just keep throwing cash in and I'll easy have 2-3 million by 2029 then leave this gay country forever.

62011846
>>62011599

62012000
>>62011574

nah

62012023

and get profit from bankrosy

OP

7-week intellectual quest.

* Up to 10k ETH prize pool.
* Pre-sale just started - 1 ETH - 25k Licenses only.
* First enigma drops Monday.

Check the contract/logic: https://awhalehunt.xyz

Early replies (first 3)

>>62009448
GOOD MORNING SAAR

62012548
>>62009448
Just 1 ETH for licence saar!

62012770
>>62009448
Take in up to 25k eth, payout up to 10k eth. Skill based, def won't be an insider scam.
I'm in!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

lmao

item.title: They flipped the switch — 42 replies — 87.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
They flipped the switch

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-03-21 05:25 UTC

OP

Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point. Well, here it is. The silent flip. Chainlink has permeated the gates of Washington and has considerable influence at this point that was built carefully and mostly out of the spotlight, because regulatory ambiguity around digital assets made overt positioning impossible.

Understand that in groups where powerful men meet and make critical decisions, Chainlink is being positioned as connective infrastructure for the next generation of finance, state-level coordination, and machine-verifiable systems. Monetary policy rails, yes we all know that. But also cyber defense architecture, government identity-adjacent workflows, and potentially even admin systems where the ability to be audited lives in the public forum.

The recent announcement from the SEC/CFTC that deems Chainlink as a commodity is the green light. I can tell you that Economics 2.0 will be announced shortly and the slides are literally being prepared as we speak. The next major milestone after that is staking expansion this summer.

There is still a question about how much friction individual states can still create on the margins (CA, NJ, SC, WI) but the decision makers don't care about that. Hope you don't live in any such places. Most people will not understand what they are looking at until after the repricing. That is how these transitions always work. By the time the headlines catch up, the asymmetry is gone.

Early replies (first 3)

62007047
WW3 is starting and you're still shilling about this gay, irrelevant scamcoin.

62007066
>>62007042
You’re going to have to get a job chief. Typing out your delusions on an indoor kite flying forum isn’t going to pay the bills. Remember, eye contact and firm handshake

62007080
Where's insider anon's larp again?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007736
>>62007042
You forgot to end with
>Heh, I'm so excited for you crazy kids!

62007869
>>62007042
Artemis-sama, how drastic will the price increase be this summer? What would be the minimum amount of link to never wage slave again and live a simple life? To make a positive impact in this world even in the slightest?

62007874
>THIS SUMMER COPE
IT WAS ALWAYS
>TONIGHT!
FOOL!

>t.Artemis

62007879
>>62007047
this world is so gay that i could see some random shitcoin like link mooning during WW3

62007881
I'm so glad this is still a chainlink board with daily posts about my favorite fat billionaire Russians business. Just kidding no I'm not please kys.

62007902
3.3 mill inflow on ETFS today.

62007905
>>62007902
the most of ANY crypto ETF today btw

62007938
so when do i sell and at what price

62007973
>>62007905
holy samefag

62008011
>>62007902
ur mom had 3.3 mill inflow today. of semen.
#rekt

62008018
>>62007387
>long-term stakers a bone?
eat shit buddy. Many of us can't even get in the pool. Be happy you've gotten the 4.5% and some random memecoins.

62008356
>>62008018
congratulations on being a latefag. you'll have to make do with sdl

62008363
>>62007042
Finally

My ducks are in a row

62008438
The copium is real in this thread. It's inevitable that we will transition to digital currency and AI oversight. You know it. I know it. It's just a matter of when. And I believe OP's prediction is a rational prediction.

62008468
>$2 above where it was when I first started researching crypto
I figured back then it'd be something I rotated gains into as it seemed to have long-term potential and it seems that's panning out better than I thought.

62008498
>>62007042
>jew id

Holy shit I think this may be a sign

62008529
>>62008498
It's a sign alright

62008569
>>62007042
artemis you suck at larping
you have no panache, no flair for drama
you have no personality

62008595
what the make it by next year stack?

62010029
>>62008569
yeah instead he talks like someone who touches grass (unlike you) and insight (also unlike you), which makes him believable. he's called things right and will be right again dumb fuck

62010118
>>62010029
>omg he's the real insider
i swear atleast one of you fags pop up for every larp. i too knew something was coming, but i kept it to myself because i hate you normal fags and your egos.

62010146
>>62007042
>She still thinks Rome happened on an investment forum
Ya'll deserve to lose money if you dont understand recent history

62010190
>>62010029
he hasn't called shit, shut the fuck up

62012054
>>62010190
>>62010118
check the archive you dummies, oh and touch grass

62012321
>>62012054
It's all fake. Proof is the chart, if any of it were true you would see being priced in.

62012392
>>62012054
>touch grass
you don't even know what your saying newfag

62012758
Price. Chart.
Adem. Won
He won. Linkies lost.

62012774
>>62012321
The longer it moves in lock step with trash like Cardano, the more I doubt.

62012804
>>62007042
> Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point.
thats me. I knew it was march. Its always march. Obvious really.

item.title: >world go bad gold must always go up >WTF WHY GOLD NO GO UP?!??! — 45 replies — 27.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>world go bad gold must always go up >WTF WHY GOLD NO GO UP?!??!

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-03-21 04:17 UTC

OP

>world go bad gold must always go up
>WTF WHY GOLD NO GO UP?!??!
Is the most brain dead retarded take imaginable. These retards fail to understand the price of gold is also linked to it's industrial use.

Early replies (first 3)

62006969
>>62006961
These are the same type of people who bought gamestop btw.

62006976
>>62006969
digits... checked

62006979
>>62006961
Probably a horde of cunts started leveraging themselves long up the ass when the war started.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007928
>>62006961
China dumping their stack to reap profits and pay for the war theyre about to join

62007949
>>62006961
Good and silver have crashed hard during an economic downturn. This is temporary. They spike back up after the money printing to keep industries running.

62007959
>>62006994
And whats in your portfolio

62009136
>>62007922
> world government tightens emissions regulations tanking the price

now what chief?

62009162
once gold goes below 2500 i may slurp

62009254
>>62006961
>but but the industrial use of gold!
makes up 10% of the usage at most

62009264
>>62006961
Markets predicted geo political uncertainty before it happened and it shot up too much. Everyone tried to do it before actual uncertainty and then bow it happened, it's falling.

62009299
>>62007949
how long does it usually take?

62009322
>>62009299
Longer than you can wait.

62009359
the markets are a casino
all you're doing is gambling
the house always wins

62009589
>>62006961
>the price of gold is also linked to it's industrial use.
Troll thread

62009613
>gold crashes to the price I last bought at
looks like it's time to buy more

62009655
Economy is fake, take this opportunity to buy more gold because the ones that want to have all the gold wants you to sell it.

62009662
>>62009359
Pretty much, you should know by now who's the house.

62009810
>>62006961
>gold has kept a high value for thousands of years
>goes down a couple of days
>HAH APPARENTLY GOLD IS WORTH EXACTLY ZERO
just go outside for a bit dude

62009821
>>62009589
yeah, before the 1700s, gold was priced at zero because it wasn't use industrially, we all know that!

62011236
>>62006961
It's time to buy,

62011262
Don't zoom out guys its over this is the all time peak for gold

62011311
The obvious answer which nobody is mentioning is that the gold price is being lowered to give banks an opportunity to reacquire physical assets instead of paper assets, in preparation so they don't get margin called, audited, etc. when the inevitable drop happens.

62011335
>>62006961
reminder even the top assets in world can/are easily rugpulled like crypto

62011402
>>62006961
This literally always happens at the beginning of a financial crisis where gold skyrockets. It's because people are retarded and dump their gold to cover their margin calls.
Gold $6000 EOY*
Silver $150 EOY*
Screenshot this.
*at least

62011417
>>62006961
>These retards fail to understand the price of gold is also linked to it's industrial use
lol
gold is saudi's money, they sell oil and buy gold, price go up, when they can't sell oil, they sell gold, price go down

62011427
>>62011335
Anon....
Tell me....

One year ago?

What was Gold and Silver prices?

62011496
what's a good site to buy physical gold/silver?

62011664
>>62011496
>>>/biz/pmg

62012196
>>62006961
>hedge against political instability
What? No, it is a hedge against inflation and currency debasement at best. A physical asset is terrible for hedging against political instability. How do you move it out of a warzone? How about out of a repressive regime?
Retard

62012210
>>62012196
CNBC claims that inflation is what is driving prices lower though.

62012310
>>62012196
Stick a gold coin up your ass. If you stick up 60 silver coins you are a tremendous faggot.

62012611
>>62012210
CNBC are disingenuous and intentionally ignore how the political instability and the need unwind leverage are the reason gold is being sold.

62012673
>>62006961
>cash out all your gold
>yolo into oil
It's retarded but it works. They're probably overleveraged to shit on paper trades

item.title: Bitcoin is being replaced — 20 replies — 13.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin is being replaced

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-21 04:23 UTC

OP

This is, with my whole hearted belief, the next Bitcoin.

Last year there were murmurs here of Bitcoin being set to be artificially under attack in 2026 in order to make way for a biometric UBI blockchain.

All wars are bankers wars.

Anon posted a website that since went down (kingtree.net) and had the archives scrubbed. On it was a forum from 2006 to 2009 that laid out the original code and implementation of Bitcoin. The code was, per the post and forum, stolen and commodified. Satoshi was there.

At the same time, the url ankh.cash was registered and dormant. Same name of the kingtree protocol (ANKH). The blockchain running since 2006 went offline, but is now live and open source on the ankh.cash url. It's been made public on February 28 2026 at 3:33am ET (numerology relevance) on Github.

I've monitored it since, and over the past two weeks, at the start of the Iran and Israel war, it began verifying biometrics and users onto the chain and distributing.

I downloaded a node and went through the biometric data. From my analysis and attempts at deciphering - it is being implemented in early stages in South America, South East Asia, and East Africa - the global south. Based on phenotypes of facial ratios.

The financial system is set to be imploded on purpose to make way for Ankh and its derivatives. Biometric blockchain based UBI for the masses - one code, one hash and tokenization of people's entire livelihood.

Early replies (first 3)

62008048
>>62008039
big if true

62008050
>>62008039
Ahhh man
*checks pants*
Yeah I've got some nasty poopie in there.
*Takes pants off and tips them upside down*
Ahhh man now it's all in your thread.

Financially speaking I this will decimate me and that's why this is on topic.

62008079
keep me posted

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62008083
>>62008039
>>62008048
>>62008050
>>62008079

samefag

62008088
>>62008083

62008092
>>62008088

62008099
>>62008083
all me

62008108
>>62008092

62008109
>>62008083
You're pointing and screaming into the void

62008323
>>62008039
When bitcoin came out, it wasn't the next anything, it was itself.

62008333
>>62008039
>capped at 9 billion
I thought it was 500m

62008341
>>62008039
Yeah say hello to those who understand face training, bone health, hormones and the light angles...woman can even play with makep and face fillers too...this cannot be just that simple

62008358
Ok so Whats the ticker ANKH is like $200 mcap cant buy it on SOL

62008512

buy it? as a goy? ahahaha
you vill have to work for zem

62008699

Sorry but you have a critical flaw in your shillcoin, theres no reason to buy

62009087

0.0
Can't tell if blessed or cursed digits

62012161

Node details:

"index": 159,
"timestamp": 1772259107285.0525,
"hash": "0xc697fe256c7ec05764ad5822dd845e41d0c91b7848c01d680f1327c98ecb88a1",

"stateRoot": "0x098d578a30f40e43f9348c5d63aef8cce289c28faabd7ca98f84c49c71167b6a",
"transactionsRoot": "0xe182ae9f084ec96a4a32f5693c97cc0643748cc4d33cd30ecd9bf3976c975c26",

{

"biometricTemplateHash": "932a461e62200978f15a8643ba0e4b06258864ca5d8e5558d1db03cdce2367dc"

62012192
Just tell me where to buy it fag

62012289

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 87 replies — 12.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-03-20 22:50 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

61933779

item.title: CORN - the next golden trade. — 15 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
CORN - the next golden trade.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-21 04:21 UTC

OP

Hormuz is shut and half the world’s urea and a third of ammonia exports are stuck behind it. Ammonia went from $850 to $1,050 a ton in three weeks, Qatar’s fertilizer plants are offline, India and Bangladesh are shutting theirs down too because they can’t get gas feedstock anymore. There’s no strategic fertilizer reserve like there is for oil, nobody planned for this. Corn needs more nitrogen than any other major crop and at these input costs it literally doesn’t make money at $4.50 a bushel, so farmers are going to rotate acres into basedbeans which fix their own nitrogen. Less corn planted means less corn harvested in September means price goes up. USDA Prospective Plantings report March 31 is the catalyst. Even if the war ends tomorrow the fertilizer still hasn’t shipped, it takes 30 days on the water plus weeks to reach inland farms, and planting season is NOW. The supply destruction is already locked in regardless of what happens in the Middle East. Pure agriculture math at this point. CORN, CXRN if you want 2x. (but it’s a small etf, just a few million dollars large) As of time of posting, you have an hour left before markets close for the weekends. Also, stop trading American oil markets and for fucks sake at least trade BNO if you insist on remaining an NPC. Good luck retards; you’re welcome.

Early replies (first 3)

62010641
I knew my piss jugs were worth saving up

62010643
Youre welcome for what? Now i got to buy corn futures to afford corn ff

62010661
> If that were true anon, wouldn’t funds be buying it?

Look at the price chart with one day candles fuckface. They are. It just hasn’t broken out yet. But it will.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62010666
What's the world going to be like when retail investors rush in to exploit commodity markets, enriching themselves in equities while simultaneously impoverishing themselves and their fellow citizens in the goods and services they buy?

62010677
>>62010643
Hey at least you’ll be eating. Whatever wealth is redefined as in post apocalyptic 2027, i want to be part of it.

62010688
>>62010666
>based digits of prophecy

I agree that the next retard retail lemming rush is into commodities of all types, and it will be awful.

62010702
>>62010688
Holy fucking trip dubs. I can’t remember the last time I saw that shit.

62012436
been saying this for a week now lol. everyone on here is obsessed with oil and shorting spy meanwhile the real play is sitting right there in the ag space. my buddy runs 800 acres in iowa and he straight up told me he’s cutting corn by 40% this year because the numbers don’t work. said his fertilizer dealer can’t even guarantee delivery dates anymore. if one guy in iowa is doing that imagine what’s happening across the entire corn belt. also nobody is talking about how china restricted their own fertilizer exports BEFORE the war even started so the global supply was already tight. this just broke it completely. i loaded up on CORN at 22 and change and im not touching it until after the usda report. might add more honestly. the fact that corn is down today while everything else is on fire just means the market hasn’t figured this out yet which is exactly when you want to be buying

62012496
>>62010635
I've seen this trade shilled on like 10 different platforms in the past 48h
>15 minutes after friday close
>trump announces we're withdrawing for good
Commodity larpers get what they deserve.

62012577
>he isn't buying corn bullion
NGMI

62012637
>>62010688
Corn is Satan's poop. If you see in yours you ought to see an exocist.

62012643
>>62010635
Whatever shall the largest gas and corn producer in the world do now?

62012653
E85 and HFCS suck nigger dick, and so will any future dipshit concoction they make from this shit.

62012678
>>62012496
>trusting trump
They jist asked for 200 billion dollars retard this might come as a surprise to you but TRUMP IS LYING
And even if he was telling the truth the strait is still closed

62012687
>>62012678
Trump is lying?
No way

item.title: Power of the ICP — 11 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Power of the ICP

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-21 04:33 UTC

OP

Made a rouge-lite space shooter arcade game on the ICP with caffeine AI. ICP to $1,000!!!

-3 ships to choose from with unique abilities and play style
-Dozens of upgrades
-cool power ups
-bosses
-leader boards
-enemy variety
-25 waves to beat the game
-prestige levels for harder and harder challenges.

This is fleshed out and polished game you can have fun with for 2-3 hrs. Works really good on tablet and smart phone. Desktop not fully tested.

https://pixel-blaster-vew.caffeine.xyz/#caffeineAdminToken=868f11e8852ffbb163253ee5f585647f53b0ebc21fdd8a314cd9c94210b2acfa

Early replies (first 3)

62007884
Nice

62007899
>>62007867
cool game, icp isnt going to pump tho. glad youre having fun making stuff, thanks for sharing.

62010116
>>62007867
I beat wave 7 on a trackpad
make it harder
remove ablity to move up and down make it like space invaders
it's pretty imbalanced as an arcade game but better than trash on icp. so keep up the good work. play some early 80s games to develop your design sensibilities.
how do you make money?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62012224
>>62010116
Waves get much harder later on, play on hard mode if too easy and unlock prestige levels by beating wave 25.

Game turns into a bullet hell and yo need to be able to move anywhere

62012231
>>62012224
Later prestige levels the early waves get difficult to kill all the enemies and you lose your “enemy clear” bonus and bonus 3rd upgrade pick. Early waves are about eliminating all enemies and getting the bonuses

62012426
>>62007867
Tech two decades ahead of time.
Yet nobody uses it, and it will fail.
It's the General Motors EV1 of blockchain.
Too much ahead of it's time.

62012450
lmfao

62012466
>>62007867
people laugh, but i'm start to believe that as internet and OS become more and more kiked with ID laws, Internet computer will become the last bastion of freedom.

62012543
Price chart cope sneethe dial 8

62012575
>>62007867
he bought something called
>internet computer
>i see pee

62012706
>>62012466
Perhaps. But it will not pump the price.
Okay let's give some advice.
Nobody gives a shit about the tech. Any tech. If it's ahead of it's time. Whatever. What count is the market. What does the market want.
Currently, it wants two things. One, it's starting to realize the importance of privacy (anonymous transactions). Secondly, it wants a very fast, and cheap (transaction fees) blockchain, to do mostly stable coin (which is a gateway to DOLLARS, not their local hyper inflated currency, in the third world).
